methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $127M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Fulfil is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $127M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of six same-stage, same-sector comparables—including 1X Technologies ($10.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Collaborative Robotics ($500M), and Fairmat ($350M)—alongside one public comparable. This peer universe reflects the heterogeneity typical of frontier robotics, with valuations spanning $300M to $10.0B.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $127M total raised as a baseline proxy and applying a typical 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier, implies a valuation of $241M. Peer-median analysis across the six same-stage robotics comparables yields a median valuation of $1.1B, though the wide range ($300M to $10.0B) reflects substantial dispersion within the cohort. No revenue or profitability data was available to support revenue-multiple or DCF approaches.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $205M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $658M. The stage-progression method anchors the lower bound; the peer median establishes the midpoint. Key uncertainties include capital efficiency relative to peers, product commercialization progress, and whether Fulfil's positioning and technology justify positioning toward the upper or lower end of the peer range. The wide dispersion in peer valuations itself suggests material sensitivity to differentiation factors not captured in stage and sector alone.
